transcript.title: Opening
transcript.text: Good morning Rushil Bhatia. Here is your daily news for Friday, July 24. In today's briefing: top headlines, bhopal and madhya pradesh, business and markets, money, tax and personal finance, science and technology, development and policy, across india, around the world. Let's begin.

transcript.title: Top headlines
transcript.text: First, the stories leading the news this morning. Sonam Wangchuk ends hunger strike; PM Modi vows stricter actions against paper leaks. Sonam Wangchuk ended his 26-day hunger strike after government assurances on student demands. Prime Minister Modi announced a new bill to combat examination paper leaks. This proposed legislation includes fast-track courts and tougher punishments for offenders. The Union Cabinet will discuss the bill on Friday before Parliament next week. Astronauts may grow Medicines: UC San Diego tests space pharma. More than half the medicines stocked on the ISS expire within three years, a serious problem for a Mars mission lasting around 400 days. A new University of California San Diego study offers a fix: grow the drugs on board. Researchers cultivated cowpea mosaic virus, a cancer-fighting plant virus, on tobacco-relative and black-eyed pea plants in simulated microgravity, then extracted it without killing the plant. 2,500 detained, homes of 2 LeT terrorists razed in Kashmir after cop killing. Security forces demolished two Lashkar-e-Taiba terrorist homes overnight in Kashmir. Over two thousand five hundred people were detained for questioning following a police killing. The operation aimed to dismantle terrorist support networks across the region. Officials reviewed security arrangements for the ongoing Amarnath Yatra pilgrimage. Pvt investment in space sector crosses $618 million: Minister Jitendra Singh to RS. India's space sector has seen unprecedented momentum and growth. Private investment has now crossed six hundred million dollars. Over four hundred active space startups are contributing to national ambitions. Indian private companies have successfully demonstrated launch and orbital capabilities. Canada college to protesting Indian students: Seek legal advice, retain imp documents. Indian students protest after Portage College's business management diploma graduates faced work visa rejections. The Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada stated their programs were not eligible for permits. Portage College encourages affected students to retain important documents and seek legal advice. The college cannot overturn decisions made by the immigration authority. Ranbir and Yash's 'Ramayana' trailer to be attached to Spider Man movie: Report. The Ramayana film's digital trailer launch has been postponed by producer Namit Malhotra. This postponement comes after its global debut at San Diego Comic-Con. Sony Pictures Entertainment is now the film's global distributor, which is exciting. Fans speculate the trailer might attach to Spider-Man: Brand New Day. US unveils fresh tariffs of up to 12.5 percent on 60 economies; rate for India lowered to 10 percent. The Trump administration on Thursday imposed new tariffs of 10 per cent and 12.5 per cent on imports from 60 economies, including India, saying the move aims to push trading partners to strengthen enforcement against goods produced using forced labour. Ships trapped near Strait of Hormuz could become nurseries for invasive species. Ships lingering in the Persian Gulf could unintentionally facilitate the spread of harmful marine invaders worldwide. Extended anchorage allows marine organisms to thrive on the hulls, creating significant biofouling concerns that threaten global maritime commerce. These invasive species can inflict extensive ecological and economic harm on coastal areas. It is imperative for nations to join forces to address biofouling issues and prevent future outbreaks of invasion. MPs ask why govt didn't inform owners of non-E20 vehicles about mileage loss. NEW DELHI: Members of the parliamentary committee on transport Thursday asked top government officials on why no effort was made to inform owners of non-E20 compliant vehicles about potential mileage loss due to use of 20 percent ethanol blended petrol in these vehicles. Florida's python hunt used 3m camera photos and found only four snakes. In Florida, scientists discovered that captive female pythons failed to attract wild males. Utilizing artificial intelligence, researchers sifted through millions of camera images, revealing few python sightings. Additionally, attempts to use mammalian scent lures for detecting the invasive Burmese pythons proved futile. However, there is optimism as removal strategies in the Everglades are progressing, bolstered by hunting and radio tracking initiatives aimed at controlling the invasive species population. Amazon cuts jobs in team focused on tech that Google, Microsoft says is their 'biggest' goals. Amazon has reduced roles within its Artificial General Intelligence unit. This restructuring impacts teams focused on model customization and data services. The company continues significant investment in artificial intelligence development and infrastructure. These job cuts follow recent leadership changes within Amazon's AI division. SC lifts 2-year-old blanket ban on setting up of industries in Taj zone. NEW DELHI: SC Thursday lifted its two-year-old blanket ban on setting up of industries in Taj Trapezium Zone, a 10,400 sq km area spread over five districts in UP and one in Rajasthan, to open avenues for employment generation.

transcript.title: Bhopal and Madhya Pradesh
transcript.text: Now, news closer to home in Bhopal and across Madhya Pradesh. Script To Appoint Jain As CEO Of NITI Aayog Written On June 12. A meeting of the governing council of NITI Aayog took place in New Delhi on June 11, and Jain stayed back in Delhi after the meeting. Ashok Barnwal Takes Over As Chief Secretary Of The State; Jain Relieved. Before becoming the chief secretary, he worked in many departments. Madhya Pradesh Government Clears Recruitment Of 9,662 Police Personnel. Recently, Chief Minister Mohan Yadav visited the PHQ to review its work, proposals and future requirements in view of Simhastha 2028 in Ujjain. Waterlogging: Only Eight Of 35 Hotspots Fixed Despite Repeated Alerts In Bhopal. The traffic police have been conducting annual surveys of congestion-prone roads for the past two years and sharing the findings with the BMC and the PWD. IIT Indore Opens Doors For 625 New PG and PhD Students. Strengthening its postgraduate academic portfolio, IIT Indore has introduced three new MTech specialisations from the academic session 2026–27: Civil Engineering with specialisation in Geotechnical Engineering, Civil Engineering with specialisation in Transportation Systems Engineerin ICAR-CIAE, Bhopal and M/s. Natures Bioplastics Pvt. Ltd. Developes Biodegradable films for Milk and water Packaging. Cong marches to BJP office in MP capital Bhopal over paper leaks, education sector issues. Zoo Struggles To Find Agency For rupees 40 Crore Aquarium Project In Indore. In view of the repeated failure to attract bidders, senior municipal officials have decided to hold a meeting this week with contractors and specialised aquarium agencies to understand the hurdles and encourage participation. Former judge, now advocate in Madhya Pradesh, debarred from practice. AIIMS Bhopal signs MoU withAIILSG to strengthen firesafety, disaster management. Potholes, Metro works trigger traffic chaos in Bhopal. Madhya Pradesh Metro Seeks Tax Relief On Depot Infrastructure. Officials from both departments held detailed discussions on the nature of various constructions and the applicable tax provisions. Madhya Pradesh to establish fire academy in Bhopal under New Fire and Emergency Services Bill, 2026. Survivors hold dharna against scan facility at gas hospital. MP secures proposed rupees 6,200cr semiconductor investment. Showers hold in city, but monsoon still active. NGT takes cognisance of Madhya Pradesh HC-mandated steps in the Indore water contamination case. MP: Students protest in Bhopal against Delhi Police action on protesters, demand Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation. CAG flags serious lapses in MP's Jal Jeevan Mission implementation. Implead IMC as water supply led to Bhagirathpura deaths: NGT panel.

transcript.title: Business and markets
transcript.text: Moving to business, the economy and the markets. CLO Price Swings Dictated by Tokyo, Wharton-Led Study Shows. The $1.3 trillion US CLO market is taking its cues from Tokyo as much as Wall Street. GIC Posts Worst Five-Year Return Since 2013; US Still Top Market. Singaporean sovereign wealth fund GIC Pte reported its lowest five-year annualized return in more than a decade, after the giant investor reduced some of its risks earlier on and its bond holdings didn't fully rebound. Heat Worries US Wheat Farmers Just as Crop Prices Surge. For farmers in North Dakota surveying their wheat as harvest closes in, scorching heat is threatening yields just as crop prices rise to a three-year high. Dollar Rises as Intensifying Iran War Lifts Rate Hike Bets. The dollar jumped Thursday as intensifying conflict in the Middle East sparked concerns over energy supply disruptions, fueling expectations that interest rates will stay higher for longer. Yen's Slump Extends Beyond Dollar and Stokes Inflation Fears. While the yen's plunge to a four-decade low against the dollar has dominated headlines, a broader gauge of the Japanese currency's health is also flashing a warning. Dollar hits new 40-year high versus yen, euro softer after ECB stands pat on rates. GLOBAL-FOREX/ (UPDATE 6, GRAPHICS):FOREX-Dollar hits new 40-year high versus yen, euro softer after ECB stands pat on rates Gold falls 2 percent as Middle East tensions fuel inflation fears, rate-hike bets. GLOBAL-PRECIOUS:Gold falls 2 percent as Middle East tensions fuel inflation fears, rate-hike bets Tesla falls 15 percent , Alphabet drops 8 percent as AI spending concerns mount. Tesla shares fell to around $319 following disappointing quarterly earnings, marking their lowest since August 2025. Alphabet also declined despite higher revenue, with concerns about rising AI investment overshadowing positive results. Both companies contributed to a 1.55 percent drop in the S and P 500. India's bike exports ride global demand surge. Bajaj Auto and TVS Motor reported strong international sales in the June quarter. Indo-MIM IPO: Issue receives solid response on Day 1, subscribed 1.4 times; NII portion booked 3 times. Indo-MIM's IPO opened on July 23 with strong demand, receiving bids for 6.29 crore shares against 4.45 crore. The non-institutional investors' interest was highest at 3.04 times. The company aims to raise rupees 500 crore while utilizing rupees 400 crore for debt repayment. ET Growth Summit: Policy, infra, innovation in focus. The summit in Ahmedabad brings founders, investors, and policymakers together to engage them in discussions about the future growth trajectory for India's MSMEs. US stocks tumble on renewed concerns over massive AI spending, rising crude prices. As of 10 a.m. Eastern Time, the S and P 500 dropped 1 percent , the Dow Jones Industrial Average was down 1 percent , and the Nasdaq Composite was 1.8 percent lower Fintechs flag NPCI default app threat to competition. Seven Indian fintech firms have written to NPCI regarding its UPI Meta protocol. IEX Q1 results: Net profit grows 12 percent YoY to rupees 135 crore; electricity volumes rise 16 percent. Indian Energy Exchange reported a robust June quarter with standalone PAT increasing 12.1 percent YoY to rupees 126.7 crore and consolidated PAT up 11.7 percent to rupees 134.8 crore. Electricity trading volume rose 15.9 percent YoY to 37.5 billion units, highlighting a strong market response during record peak demand. Delhi Police say no crackdown tonight at protest. Delhi Police has denied any plans for a crackdown on the Jantar Mantar protest. Sebi proposes faster online dispute resolution, bigger role for exchanges and depositories. The market regulator is seeking to speed up investor grievance redressal with tighter timelines, stronger enforcement and a revamped arbitration framework. Second chokepoint piles pressure on India's oil. India faces intensified energy supply challenges due to choke point closures. AMC profits rise on stronger core business and treasury gains. Profit after tax rose 12-27 percent year-on-year in Q1FY27, aided by buoyant equity markets that lifted assets under management through mark-to-market gains. Government extends mobile internet ban at Jantar Mantar. Mobile internet services were suspended near Jantar Mantar until midnight on Thursday. Sebi proposes MF-only portfolio management service, wider investment universe. Under the proposal, portfolio managers would be allowed to exclusively manage client money in direct plans of mutual fund schemes, including exchange-traded funds and specialized investment funds, through a separate MF CJP leaders to meet govt ministers tomorrow. A Cockroach Janta Party delegation will meet Union ministers on Friday. SBI Mutual Fund trims stake in Ather Energy to 5 percent after partial profit booking. Details here. SBI Mutual Fund has trimmed its stake to 5 percent in Ather Energy through partial profit booking. The fund house has sold 6,16,733 shares, representing 0.1565 percent of the paid-up share capital of Ather Energy. Delhi Metro closures, July 24: 17 stations shut. Delhi Metro station closures: Seventeen Delhi Metro stations will remain closed from 7.30 am on Friday, July 24, due to heightened security around the ongoing CJP protest. Lohia Corp IPO: Issue receives modest response on Day 1, subscribed 0.40 times; GMP signals muted listing. Lohia Corp's IPO saw modest demand on Day 1, with bids for 56.8 lakh shares against an offer of 1.43 crore shares, achieving 0.40 times subscription. The IPO continues until July 27, with a price band of rupees 404-425 per share. Nandini butter and ghee prices hiked. Bengaluru Cooperative Milk Union has increased prices for Nandini ghee and butter. Newly-listed SME stock DSM Fresh Foods jumps over 30 percent in 2 days after reporting a 58 percent YoY jump in Q1 revenue. SME stock DSM Fresh Foods surged nearly 32 percent in just two consecutive sessions, defying weak market sentiment, after the company reported a 58 percent year-on-year (YoY) jump in its revenue to rupees 74 crore for Q1FY27. FDA: 1.6 mln dozen eggs recalled across US. FDA Recalled Eggs: Nearly 1.6 million dozen eggs have been recalled due to potential Salmonella Enteritidis contamination. Oil price soars close to $100/barrel on fresh Mideast attacks, fears over war widening. Main oil contract Brent North Sea surged toward $100 a barrel today as Tehran-backed Houthi rebels targeted Red Sea shipping, raising fears that the Middle East war is widening. CJP protest puts Jantar Mantar's history in focus. Jantar Mantar, built in 1731, served as an astronomical observatory for centuries.

transcript.title: Money, tax and personal finance
transcript.text: Now to your money — taxes, savings and personal finance. NRE versus NRO FD: SBI, HDFC Bank, PNB and Axis Bank interest rates compared; know key differences. NRE and NRO accounts offer investment avenues for Non-Resident Indians in India. NRO accounts accept Indian earnings, while NRE accounts hold international funds. Interest on NRO deposits faces tax, unlike NRE accounts which are tax-free. NRE funds are fully repatriable, and NRO funds have a yearly repatriation limit. Have unclaimed insurance and EPF amounts? Check where to make claims online under 'Your Money , Your Right' campaign. Today we explain how investors with unclaimed LIC insurance amounts or inoperative EPF accounts can check their dues and make claims online facilitated by the Centre's 'Your Money , Your Right' campaign. How to file ITR-3 online on the Income Tax e-filing portal for AY 2026-2027. Individuals with business income must file ITR-3 for AY 2026-2027. This form captures various income sources, including salary and capital gains. Taxpayers can file ITR-3 online through the e-filing portal. The process involves filling general information and specific schedules. Son becomes NRI, gifts shares worth rupees 30 lakh to father; should this be disclosed in ITR? What taxpayers must do to avoid tax notices. NRI son gifted rupees 30 lakh shares to his father through an off-market transfer. Should it be reported in ITR? Although a genuine gift of shares from a son to his father is not regarded as a transfer for capital gains purposes in the hands of the donor, the reporting of such off-market transactions in the Annual Information Statement (AIS) often creates uncertainty. Experts explain AIS, tax rules and how to avoid notices. Provident Fund: Forgot your UAN? Here's how to instantly recover your PF number online. Assigned by the EPFO, the UAN is a permanent 12-digit code that stays with you throughout your professional career. Pay commission: West Bengal govt appoints former IAS officer Atanu Chakraborty as Chairperson of 7th state CPC. The West Bengal government has constituted the state's 7th central pay commission and appointed former IAS officer Atanu Chakraborty as Chairman of the panel. Here are the latest updates from the state... Need help with ITR filing? Check the ITR filing charges across various websites for AY 2026-2027 for students, salaried persons, pensioners and NRIs. Individuals can compare income tax return filing charges across several platforms. The deadline for filing is July 31, 2026, for many taxpayers. AI-assisted self-filing options are available at lower costs. Expert-assisted plans offer comprehensive support for complex income situations. Punjab HC reserves judgement on Punjab govt, state power corporation's pleas seeking stay on DA release order. The Punjab HC reserved judgement on pleas filed by the state and PSPCL against a previous court order directing release of Dearness Allowance for state employees and pensioners by 30 June. Here's all we know... FCNR (B) versus NRE 5-year FD: Which can give higher maturity on $50,000 investment? Know currency risk. FCNR(B) versus NRE currency risk: FCNR(B) and NRE deposits offer distinct investment avenues for non-resident Indians. FCNR(B) deposits provide currency protection, while NRE deposits carry exchange rate risk. Calculations show NRE deposits can yield higher returns if the rupee strengthens. However, the rupee has historically depreciated against the US dollar. Foreign salary in NRE account not taxable for NRIs, ITAT quashes rupees 12 lakh tax demand. The ITAT Ahmedabad has ruled that foreign salary earned outside India does not become taxable merely because it is credited to an NRE account or because TDS is deducted using an Indian TAN. The cost investors pay for chasing growth. The hope is that enough companies exceed expectations and justify lofty valuations, offsetting those that stumble. But the real risk lies not in company performance, but in how we allocate capital. Can a shop charge you extra for paying by credit card? Here's what every card user should know. Can merchants legally charge customers extra for credit card payments? The Reserve Bank of India regulates these payment systems and card acceptance rules. Consumers should check final amounts and report unauthorized transactions immediately. Can children claim a share in property inherited by their father? Karnataka HC explains when they can, and cannot. The Karnataka High Court ruling clarifies that property received by a father from his own father does not automatically become ancestral. Children cannot claim a birthright in such assets if they remain the father's self-acquired property. EPS pension calculator: How much monthly pension can you get after 10 years of service under EPS 2026?. EPS pension : The central government has implemented the Employees' Pension Scheme 2026. This new scheme replaces the Employees' Family Pension Scheme 1971 and EPS 1995. Pension calculation formula remains unchanged while new rules address fund investment and claim delays. Cash deposits below rupees 10 lakh can still attract an Income Tax notice. Here's why. The rupees 10 lakh threshold under Rule 114E only determines when banks must report cash deposits through the Statement of Financial Transactions (SFT). It does not prevent the Income Tax Department from seeking information about deposits below that limit where required under the Income-tax Act. ITR filing: Received Section 143(1) intimation with tax demand? Respond within 30 days or risk being treated as an 'assessee in default'; know how to do it. Taxpayers must respond to Section 143(1) intimations with tax demands promptly. Failure to respond within thirty days can lead to penalties. Interest accrues on unpaid demands after the stipulated period expires. Ignoring these notices may result in being classified as an assessee in default. Section 44ADA: How freelancers and professionals can legally pay tax only on 50 percent of their total income. Section 44ADA allows eligible professionals to pay tax on 50 percent of their gross receipts as taxable income, treating the rest as a deemed expense deduction. The scheme applies to specified professionals with receipts up to rupees 50 lakh, extendable to rupees 75 lakh if cash receipts are within 5 percent . Over 3 crore IT rupees already filed: What happens if you miss 31 July deadline? Tax experts explain. With more than 3 crore Income Tax Returns already filed, taxpayers have a limited time before the 31 July 2026 deadline. Missing it can lead to late-filing fees, interest, refund delays, psychological stress, and loss of tax benefits, making timely filing essential. ​Need an interest certificate for ITR? Here's how to download it from SBI, HDFC Bank, Axis Bank and India Post​. If you earned interest from savings accounts, fixed deposits, recurring deposits or post office deposits during the financial year 25-26, you may need an interest certificate while filing your Income Tax Return (ITR). Nifty Smallcap index saw over 20 percent intra-year declines in 12 of 20 years: What history reveals about timing the market. Small-cap investing has historically delivered strong return potential but with frequent sharp corrections. Data from Nifty Smallcap 250 TRI show that staying invested through volatility and adopting disciplined approaches, such as SIPs, reduces the impact of market timing on long-term outcomes. New tax regime: Save up to rupees 65,500 in tax through your employer's NPS contribution; here's how to claim it. Section 80CCD(2) provides a tax deduction for employer contributions to NPS. This benefit remains available under the new tax regime for salaried individuals. Private sector employees switching to the new regime find this deduction particularly attractive. Employer contributions lower taxable income and build retirement savings simultaneously. Forgot your UAN? Here's how to retrieve it online through the EPFO portal. The Universal Account Number (UAN) is essential for checking your PF balance, filing claims and accessing other EPFO services. Here's how members can recover a forgotten UAN online or through EPFO's offline facilities. GPF interest rate for government employees: Has Finance Ministry changed GPF rate from 7.1 percent for July-September 2026 quarter?. The Ministry of Finance has announced the GPF interest rate for the upcoming quarter. General Provident Fund and similar government funds will earn 7.1 percent interest. This rate remains unchanged for the July to September 2026 period. Public Provident Fund and small savings schemes also retain their current rates. EPF versus mutual funds: Why EPFO says your provident fund should remain foundation of retirement planning. EPFO has urged salaried employees not to withdraw EPF savings for mutual funds, highlighting EPF's retirement, pension and insurance benefits. Here's how EPF differs from mutual funds, key features, tax benefits and practical investment takeaways for long-term financial planning. EPF Scheme 2026 rules: Major changes every EPF member and employer must know. The Employees' Provident Funds Scheme, 2026, notified by the Ministry of Labour and Employment on June 29 and effective from July 1, 2026, brings together various reforms that the EPFO has rolled out over the past two years.While the 12 percent EPF contribution rate and the rupees 15,000 statutory wage ceiling remain unchanged, the new scheme has introduced important changes affecting PF withdrawals, nominations and employer compliance. Can an insurer reject your claim over a tax issue? Consumer panel answers. A Rajasthan consumer commission has reportedly ruled that insurers cannot reject an otherwise valid claim merely because of an unrelated tax irregularity. The decision underlines that the reason for claim repudiation must have a direct link with the insured loss. ETF split FAQs: Why fund houses are splitting units and how investors benefit. A split reduces the per-unit price of an ETF and increases the number of units an investor owns, while the total value of the investment remains the same. It is not a taxable event and tax applies only when the investor sells the ETF units.

transcript.title: Science and technology
transcript.text: Next, science and technology. Nvidia CEO Huang says 'Taiwan saved the American semiconductor industry', and without it, Japan could have taken over. Huang noted that Taiwan companies' choices stemmed from strategic business judgment and long-standing corporate relationships rather than external coercion. Alexa Plus is getting an AI update to handle more complicated instructions. Amazon is launching an update to its Alexa Plus assistant that will allow it to connect to smart home devices in new ways. With the update, which is currently in preview, Alexa Plus can link up with tech from Bosch, Delta, Ecovacs, iRobot, Yale Home, Whirlpool, Tapo, Eufy, and others, while automatically routing requests to Meet the judges who will crown Australia's next breakout startup. TechCrunch Startup Battlefield is coming to Australia , and we're partnering with Stripe to find the country's most exciting early-stage startups. Orcas filmed smashing giant sunfish into thousands of pieces. Researchers in the Gulf of California have documented a dramatic orca behavior never described before. In two separate encounters, one orca gripped a dead sunfish by the fin while another charged into it at high speed, blasting the carcass into thousands of pieces. iPhone 18 Pro launching soon: Expected price, camera, display, processor and more. Apple is set to launch the iPhone 18 Pro lineup soon with new color options, a smaller Dynamic Island, and advanced LTPO+ OLED technology. The Echo Show 21 is a great smart home hub that's $80 off. Split between buying a smart calendar, a kitchen TV, a smart home hub, and a smart display? Amazon's Echo Show 21 is all of those things in one, with a huge 21-inch screen. You can use it to control your smart lights, glance at recipes, watch TV shows, and more. Currently, Best Buy and Home Mobileye CEO Amnon Shashua to step aside as company pushes into robotaxis, robotics. Shashua has been invited to take the chairman of the board seat. Google makes switching from iPhone to Android easier with Android 17: Here's how. Google is making it easier to switch from iOS to Android with the new Android Switch experience. FCC Chairman Brendan Carr's war on the First Amendment. As the chairman of the Federal Communications Commission, Brendan Carr has authority over the nation's TV, radio, and internet. But since Donald Trump was elected to his second term, Carr has wielded that power to threaten broadcasters that have exercised their free speech rights to make jokes about the president. He was even able to AMD takes on Nvidia with its Helios AI rack-scale system. AMD is challenging its chipmaker rival with a new rack-scale system that will start shipping to customers later this year. Google Pixel 11 series all colous leaked ahead of launch: Check expected specifications and priceGoogle Pixel 11 series. Google will unveil the Pixel 11 lineup at its Made by Google event on August 12. Leaks show four models in various colors, featuring a triple rear camera setup and powered by the new Tensor G6 chipset Claude's voice mode is now available for Opus and Sonnet. Until now, voice mode has only been available on Claude Haiku, Anthropic's faster but less powerful model. Now the company is making its Opus and Sonnet models available in voice mode, and extending its reach into apps like Gmail, Slack, and Canva. When Anthropic launched voice mode last year, it was primarily focused on delivering Patreon lays off 20 percent of its workforce. In a memo to staff that was posted online, Conte said the company's core business is strong, but the platform has to respond to market changes and adjust its cost structure to remain stable. Antarctica may give the world decades to prepare for rising seas. Scientists may be able to reliably forecast Antarctic ice loss through the middle of this century, offering governments a vital head start on sea level rise. After that, unstable ice retreat could accelerate dramatically, making the future much harder to predict. US Department of Energy announces first projects under Genesis Mission RFA to accelerate AI-driven scientific breakthroughs. The US Department of Energy (DOE) has announced the first projects selected under the Genesis Mission Request for Applications (RFA), as per a statement by US DOE. Patreon is laying off 20 percent of workers. Patreon is laying off 20 percent of its workers, or around 93 employees, as reported earlier by 404 Media. In a memo to employees, Patreon CEO Jack Conte writes that the company isn't making these changes "because we believe AI replaces humans," but says AI has "fundamentally transformed the tech industry, including how we work, Insurance startup Corgi reportedly raised more money at $4B , its third round in 8 weeks. In the AI-funding frenzy, many startups are raising back-to-back rounds at ever-increasing valuations , but even by those standards, Corgi stands out. Popular childhood drinks may raise blood pressure decades later. A 25-year study found that frequent consumption of soda, sports drinks, and fruit juice beginning in childhood was linked to a higher risk of high blood pressure in adulthood. Swapping one daily drink for whole fruit, milk, or water could significantly reduce that risk. Corsair's PC case with a panoramic glass design is $70 off. On the hunt for a PC case that's loaded with features and has a unique look? Amazon, Best Buy, and Corsair are selling the Corsair Frame 4500X RS for $119.99, a $70 discount from the usual price. The versatile case supports motherboards up to EATX, GPUs up to 460mm in length, and has a seamless Tesla's door handles may spur new US safety rules. The new rule-making process follows a series of incidents, including fatal ones, in which people have become stuck inside cars. Scientists discover the hidden brain switch that assigns bees their jobs. Researchers found that specific brain circuits help decide which jobs bees perform as they age. When those circuits were switched off, older worker bees began caring for the queen again instead of carrying out their normal later-life duties. The findings reveal that a bee colony's complex division of labor may be guided by neural switches hidden inside each bee's brain. Tesla's robotaxi promises are clashing with reality. In an earnings call yesterday, Tesla CEO Elon Musk did his best to paint a positive portrait of the company's robotaxi program. New cities are being added, more miles are being driven, and more people are experiencing Tesla's unsupervised vehicles. But Musk's typical bullishness seemed to be absent from the call, as the occasional trillionaire Meta drops out of a major clean energy pact as its natural gas buildout accelerates. Meta has made significant investments in natural gas over the past year. Now it's dropping out of an industry renewable energy group. NASA will test SpaceX and Blue Origin Moon landers before the next lunar landing. NASA is preparing for future astronaut landings on the Moon with an unusually ambitious orbital rehearsal. During Artemis III in 2027, Orion astronauts will rendezvous and dock separately with prototype lunar landers from Blue Origin and SpaceX, allowing crews and ground teams to test the complex maneuvers needed for later missions. Geekbench 7 will push your computer or phone even harder for better benchmarking. Primate Labs is releasing Geekbench 7, the latest generation of its popular benchmarking tool. Geekbench 7 features new video and audio encoding / decoding tests, a redesigned multi-core test, and larger, more demanding datasets for CPUs and GPUs to crunch through. It otherwise looks and acts just like Geekbench 6, and a Pro license will Anthropic updates Claude voice mode with more capable models. Claude's new voice model will let you reschedule your meeting or draft an email.

transcript.title: Development and policy
transcript.text: Now to development, infrastructure and government policy. Does India's dependency on US dollars make the economy vulnerable?. In India, Cancer Is Increasingly Costing Survivors Their Livelihoods. India's CJP Protests Meet Internet Shutdowns and Pervasive Surveillance. India's Student Protests Test Modi's Politics of Silence. Project Kusha, India's desi S-400, takes to the skies; maiden test flight successful. WTO members question India's PLI scheme, tariffs and QCO regime. India and Georgia: Deepening historic bonds through education, tourism and cultural diplomacy. India names Anurag Jain as NITI Aayog CEO. Indian Airlines Expanding Domestic and International Routes. Sri Lanka asks India for more funding after digital ID bids exceed grant. India faces scrutiny over tariffs, MSP, QCOs at WTO review.

transcript.title: Across India
transcript.text: Turning to the big national stories. No Parliament business, four days into monsoon session. Ongoing protests, P 22 Pvt investment in space sector crosses $618 million, 17 startups given nod for space activities: Minister Jitendra Singh to RS. India's space sector has seen unprecedented momentum and growth. Private investment has now crossed six hundred million dollars. Over four hundred active space startups are contributing to national ambitions. Indian private companies have successfully demonstrated launch and orbital capabilities. Reached out 4 times, says govt; talks at Jantar Mantar or a neutral venue, says CJP. NEW DELHI: Government made a fresh outreach Thursday to agitating students for talks over the issues being raised by them, emphasising that a solution to the crisis cannot be found without dialogue. Sonam Wangchuk ends hunger strike; PM Modi assures more stricter actions on paper leaks- top developments. Sonam Wangchuk ended his 26-day hunger strike after government assurances on student demands. Prime Minister Modi announced a new bill to combat examination paper leaks. This proposed legislation includes fast-track courts and tougher punishments for offenders. The Union Cabinet will discuss the bill on Friday before Parliament next week. Sonam Wangchuk breaks hunger strike after 26 days. Activist Sonam Wangchuk ended his hunger strike past midnight after 26 days at Medanta hospital in Gurgaon. 'In view of possible violence in the country': Sonam Wangchuk ends hunger strike after 26 days; PM Modi 'prays for his health'. Sonam Wangchuk ended his 26-day hunger strike after prolonged negotiations and to prevent potential violence. Prime Minister Narendra Modi announced tougher measures against examination paper leaks. The Union Cabinet will consider stricter punishments and fast-track courts for offenders. The government aims to introduce legislation during the ongoing Monsoon Session of Parliament. Won't forget Indian military's rescue of 12 Chinese crew members after ship fire: China's defence attache. NEW DELHI: China's newly appointed Defence Attache to India, Rear Admiral You Jian, has thanked the Indian military for rescuing 12 Chinese crew members from a cargo ship that caught fire last year, saying Beijing "will not forget" the operation. 400 Pakistani handles revived from Op Sindoor target NEET protests, being blocked: Delhi Police. Delhi Police identified and blocked over 400 Pakistani social media handles. These accounts are spreading misinformation and rumors amid ongoing protests. The same handles were active during Operation Sindoor and aim to provoke people. Police urged citizens, especially students, not to fall for fake content. Omar Abdullah to JP Nadda: Right on J and K leak, wrong on facts. SRINAGAR: J and K chief minister Omar Abdullah rejected Thursday Union minister JP Nadda's claim linking "National Conference (NC) and Congress" to a govt job exam leak in the Union territory, pointing out that the fiasco occurred in 2022 under the lieutenant governor's (LG) administration when his govt was not in place. Police, SDRF rescue 2 trapped in Tawi river; Army launches relief ops in Reasi, Rajouri. JAMMU: A joint team of J and K Police and SDRF rescued two individuals stranded in the Tawi near Prambli, in Jammu's Phallian mandal, following a sudden rise in the river's water level Thursday, even as the Army launched relief and evacuation operations in rain-hit Reasi and Rajouri districts. 'More strict actions to come against paper leaks': PM Modi announces new Bill push in video message. NEW DELHI: Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Thursday announced that government will bring in more strict actions against paper leaks. CJP delegation to meet government ministers on Friday as Centre renews outreach: Report. NEW DELHI: A delegation of the Cockroach Janata Party (CJP) will meet government ministers at 12.30 pm on Friday, PTI reported, citing sources, marking the first formal engagement after days of deadlock between the Centre and the student-led outfit over the alleged NEET paper leak. Government reshuffles top bureaucrats in ministries: Education secretary shifted out. NEW DELHI: The Centre on Thursday carried out a major bureaucratic reshuffle, effecting 13 secretary-level appointments across ministries and departments, including key changes in the education ministry, amid continuing protests over the alleged NEET paper leak.

transcript.title: Around the world
transcript.text: And finally, here is what is happening around the world. India activist ends hunger strike after 26 days. Sonam Wangchuk, a supporter of youth-led protesters demanding education reforms, says he wants to avert "possible violence". Ukraine's ousted defence minister insists on being reinstated. Mykhailo Fedorov refuses President Zelensky's offer to accept an alternative post, as the battle of wills continues. Yemen's Houthis attack Saudi tanker as US launches more Iran strikes. The Iran-backed group, which previously announced a "maritime embargo" against Saudi Arabia, also claims to have hit another vessel.

transcript.title: Sign-off
transcript.text: That is your briefing. Stay sharp, and have a strong day.
